Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given two POWL models of a Hire to Retire process Lets first recall what a standard Hire to Retire process might look like Typically the sequence is 1 Post a job advertisement 2 Screen candidates 3 Conduct interviews 4 Make a hiring decision 5 Onboard the new employee 6 Add the employee to the payroll system 7 Close the case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is a linear sequence but note that sometimes there might be parallel activities or choices However in the standard model the order is largely sequential with some dependencies you cannot screen before posting interview before screening decide before interviewing onboard before deciding add to payroll before onboarding or maybe after onboarding but typically payroll is set up before or during onboarding and close only after payroll is set up

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We note that there is no edge from Interview to Decide Instead we have Screen to Decide so Decide can happen after Screening without necessarily having conducted interviews Screen to Interview so Interview can happen after Screening but there is no order between Interview and Decide

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This means that in Model 1 the hiring decision Decide can be made without conducting interviews which is a severe anomaly because typically you interview before making a decision

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Also note that Interview and Decide are both after Screen but they are not ordered with respect to each other So it is possible to do Interview and Decide in any order or even in parallel but since there is no edge from Interview to Decide it is possible to Decide without having done Interview This is a significant deviation from the norm

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Here Onboard is after Decide which is correct But the missing edge between Interview and Decide is a problem

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: That means do Onboard then either exit the loop or do skip and then Onboard again This loop can run multiple times However in the context of onboarding it might be unusual to repeat the onboarding activity Onboarding is typically done once per employee So having a loop on onboarding is an anomaly because it allows multiple onboardings

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is an exclusive choice we can either do Payroll or skip it That means adding to payroll is optional

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is a severe anomaly because every employee must be added to payroll Making payroll optional would mean some employees are not paid which is not acceptable

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Post to Screen and Post to Interview This means both Screening and Interview can happen after Posting and they are not ordered with each other So it is possible to conduct interviews without screening because there is no edge from Screen to Interview

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is an anomaly because typically you screen candidates before interviewing

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Interview to Decide This is good as the decision should come after interviews

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 anomalies 1 The hiring decision can be made without conducting interviews because there is no edge from Interview to Decide This is a severe anomaly because it violates the core logic of hiring you should interview before deciding 2 Interview and Decide are both after Screen but they are not ordered so they could happen in any order This might allow Decide before Interview which is not standard

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 anomalies 1 The onboarding activity can be repeated loop This is not standard because onboarding is typically a one time activity per hire 2 Payroll is optional XOR with skip This is a severe anomaly because every employee must be added to payroll 3 There is no order between Screen and Interview so Interview can happen without Screening

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We must consider the severity of the anomalies

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: In Model 1 the process can be completed without an interview which is not acceptable In Model 2 the process can be completed without adding the employee to payroll which is also not acceptable

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In Model 2 the interview is ordered before the decision so that part is better But in Model 2 the payroll step is optional while in Model 1 the process requires that after onboarding we do payroll and then close So payroll is mandatory in Model 1

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In Model 1 screening is enforced before interview because there is an edge from Screen to Interview In Model 2 there is no edge from Screen to Interview so Interview can happen without Screen

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 has one major anomaly decision can be before interview Model 2 has three anomalies optional payroll repeated onboarding and no screening before interview

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore Model 1 is closer to normative because it only has one severe anomaly and it enforces screening before interview and mandatory payroll

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Model 1 has one critical anomaly missing order between Interview and Decide but enforces other important orders Screening before Interview and Decide and mandatory Payroll Model 2 enforces the order between Interview and Decide but has three anomalies two of which optional Payroll and no order between Screen and Interview are critical to the integrity of the process

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore Model 1 more closely aligns with a normative Hire to Retire process because it ensures that payroll is mandatory and that screening is a prerequisite for both interview and decision whereas Model 2 allows critical steps to be skipped or reordered in a way that violates standard hiring practices

Name: Validation - PE
Text: However it is important to note that neither model is fully normative A normative model would enforce Post Screen Interview Decide Onboard Payroll Close with no loops on onboarding and no optional payroll But between the two Model 1 is closer to this normative sequence
